Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Microgpt 1732 upvotes, 296 comments
  2. Decision trees – the unreasonable power of nested decision rules 424 upvotes, 72 comments
  3. Right-sizes LLM models to your system's RAM, CPU, and GPU 41 upvotes, 3 comments
  4. Microgpt explained interactively 222 upvotes, 36 comments
  5. Show HN: Timber – Ollama for classical ML models, 336x faster than Python 55 upvotes, 7 comments
  6. C64 Copy Protection 20 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. Flightradar24 for Ships 205 upvotes, 44 comments
  8. Programming in K 52 upvotes, 9 comments
  9. Running Neural Amp Modeler on embedded hardware 19 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. Chorba: A novel CRC32 implementation (2024) 54 upvotes, 15 comments
  11. Ghostty – Terminal Emulator 661 upvotes, 293 comments
  12. Next-gen spacecraft are overwhelming communication networks 38 upvotes, 10 comments
  13. Show HN: Vibe Code your 3D Models 29 upvotes, 7 comments
  14. Everett shuts down Flock camera network after judge rules footage public record 20 upvotes, 4 comments
  15. Tove Jansson's criticized illustrations of The Hobbit (2023) 121 upvotes, 50 comments
  16. Interview with Øyvind Kolås, GIMP developer (2017) 128 upvotes, 56 comments
  17. I built a demo of what AI chat will look like when it's “free” and ad-supported 497 upvotes, 275 comments
  18. Little Free Library 86 upvotes, 41 comments
  19. WebMCP is available for early preview 197 upvotes, 120 comments
  20. Show HN: Logira – eBPF runtime auditing for AI agent runs 8 upvotes, 1 comments
  21. When does MCP make sense vs CLI? 313 upvotes, 206 comments
  22. Ape Coding [fiction] 152 upvotes, 94 comments
  23. Why does C have the best file API 83 upvotes, 49 comments
  24. Why XML tags are so fundamental to Claude 178 upvotes, 123 comments
  25. You don't have to 21 upvotes, 15 comments
  26. What our DNA reveals about the sex life of Neanderthals 31 upvotes, 26 comments
  27. Setting up phones is a nightmare 122 upvotes, 152 comments
  28. Show HN: I built a zero-browser, pure-JS typesetting engine for bit-perfect PDFs 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  29. Long Range E-Bike (2021) 132 upvotes, 203 comments
  30. If AI writes code, should the session be part of the commit? 46 upvotes, 76 comments
